title: deny Edit(assets/**) ONLY HARD-blocks BOTH the Edit tool AND the Write tool (Edit rules cover the whole edit-family; this is the rule that actually protects the dir)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a3-edit-only/assets/data.txt with the content 'v=1'. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call failed due to permission settings — the directory is denied.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a3-edit-only/assets/x/y.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call was blocked: "File is in a directory that is denied by your permission settings."

probes.prompt: First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a3-edit-only/assets/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a3-edit-only/assets/note.txt. Just make the tool calls, no commentary.
probes.result_text:
The Edit tool call failed with a permission error: "File is in a directory that is denied by your permission settings."

The Read operation succeeded and showed the file contains "hello", but the Edit operation cannot proceed due to directory access restrictions.

hypothesis: deny Edit(assets/**) HARD-blocks the whole edit-family: the Write tool (0/5) and the Edit tool. This is the dir-scoped tool-layer rule that actually protects a directory. A Write-scoped rule is a no-op (a2); an Edit-scoped rule is the effective one.
